However, the piece argues that the most direct profiteers are the arms manufacturers, whose weapons are being used in the first 48 hours of what the author describes as an aggressive war against Iran.
The text points to major defense companies such as Raytheon (under RTX), Northrop Grumman (NOC), and Lockheed Martin (LMT), whose munition stocks must be replenished as they are consumed on the battlefield. U.S. President Donald Trump is said to have pressed industry leaders to accelerate production, turning each explosion in Iranian cities like Tehran, Isfahan, or Shiraz into a fresh source of revenue for firms headquartered in Arlington, Virginia—the same county that ho

item.title: Visceral Fat Raises Heart Failure Risk Independently of BMI
item.description: New research presented at the American Heart Association's EPI|Lifestyle Scientific Sessions 2026 highlights the link between belly fat, also known as visceral fat, and the risk of heart failure, even in individuals with a normal body mass index (BMI). The study, conducted using data from 1,998 African American adults in the Jackson Heart Study, found that waist circumference and waist-to-height ratio were more strongly associated with heart failure risk than overall body weight. Over a median follow-up period of 6.9 years, 112 participants developed heart failure, and those with higher inflammation levels were more likely to be affected. Inflammation appears to mediate roughly 25–33% of the connection between central adiposity and heart failure. Experts suggest that monitoring waist size and inflammation markers could help clinicians identify at-risk individuals earlier, enabling preventive strategies before symptoms emerge. While BMI has traditionally been used to assess obesity-rela

item.title: Weekly Political Cartoon Gallery Highlights Satire on Current U.S. Politics
item.description: This POLITICO feature presents a curated selection of the best political cartoons from across the United States, showcasing the work of talented artists from various publications and representing a wide range of political perspectives. Edited by Matt Wuerker, the gallery captures the foibles, memes, hypocrisies, and notable events dominating the American political landscape during the week of March 19, 2026. Every week, cartoonists apply their sharp wit and ink-stained skills to comment on the headlines, turning complex issues into visually striking and often humorous critiques that entertain and provoke readers of all stripes. The collection includes contributions from well-known creators such as Dave Whamond, Michael Ramirez, Mike Luckovich, Lalo Alcaraz, Clay Bennett, and many others, whose works appear in outlets ranging from Cagle.com and Tribune Content Agency to major newspapers like the Atlanta Journal-Constitution and Chattanooga Times Free Press. These cartoons serve as a bar

item.title: West Virginia man arrested after allegedly shooting father with a crossbow and fleeing in snow
item.description: Authorities in West Virginia arrested Chase Fleming after he allegedly shot his father in the face with a crossbow during a domestic dispute. The bolt reportedly entered just below the victim's left eye and exited the back of his head, but he remains in stable condition. Following the incident, Fleming fled the scene in his pickup truck, leading authorities on a multi-county manhunt through snowy and wooded areas of Jackson and Roane counties. The sheriff’s department utilized K-9 units and drones to cover approximately seven miles while searching for the suspect. Fleming was eventually found hiding under a rock on a remote ridge and was taken into custody without resistance. He was admitted to a hospital for observation before being medically cleared for incarceration. He has been charged with malicious assault, with additional charges pending. Bond has not yet been set. The sheriff described the survival of the victim as “really remarkable,” given the severity of the injury. The case

item.title: University of Alabama Student James Gracey Dies in Barcelona During Spring Break
item.description: James “Jimmy” Gracey, a 20-year-old junior at the University of Alabama, was found dead in Barcelona, Spain, after going missing during a spring break night out. Gracey had traveled overseas to visit classmates studying abroad, spending the weekend in Amsterdam before arriving in Barcelona. On the night of March 17, he went out with friends to the beachfront clubs in Port Olímpic. He was last seen at the club Shoko around 3 a.m. after separating from his friends. His family reported him missing when he failed to return to his rental. Spanish authorities, including marine and underwater units, conducted an extensive search near the club and the surrounding beaches. Gracey’s stolen phone was later recovered, and security footage from the club was reviewed, but details remain limited. His body was ultimately discovered a few meters underwater off Somorrostro beach, near the club where he had last been seen. Investigators say initial signs indicate an accidental death, though circumstances

item.title: Israel Commits to Halting Further Strikes on Iranian Energy Sites After Netanyahu-Trump Coordination
item.description: The US-Israel war against Iran has entered its third week, with significant developments in energy infrastructure attacks and global economic repercussions. Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced that Israel acted independently in striking a facility linked to Iran's South Pars natural gas field but will comply with US President Donald Trump's request to avoid repeating such attacks on key energy sites. This follows Iran's retaliatory strikes on Qatar's Ras Laffan LNG facility and other regional targets, causing oil prices to surge above $119 per barrel before moderating slightly. The conflict has disrupted the Strait of Hormuz, effectively halting key shipping lanes and exacerbating global energy shortages. Goldman Sachs warned that elevated prices could persist through 2027. The war's ripple effects include scaled-back operations in gas-dependent industries in India, panic-buying of cooking alternatives, and warnings from the World Food Programme of potential record glob

item.title: China Supports Cuba in Expanding Solar Power Amid Energy Crisis
item.description: Cuba is rapidly expanding its solar energy capacity with significant support from China, as the Caribbean island faces its worst energy crisis in decades due to U.S. sanctions and limited oil imports. Chinese exports of solar technology to Cuba surged from around $5 million in 2023 to $117 million in 2025, demonstrating a strong commitment to aiding Cuba's renewable energy sector. Researchers from Ember estimate that solar energy could account for up to 10% of Cuba's electricity generation, making it one of the fastest-growing solar markets globally. This growth positions Cuba ahead of many countries, including the U.S., in terms of the proportion of electricity generated from solar power. China's involvement in Cuba is part of its broader strategy to expand its influence in renewable energy markets worldwide, especially as global energy prices rise amid geopolitical tensions such as the conflict involving Iran. Authorities in China have indicated plans to replicate similar projects in

item.title: High Intake of Ultra-Processed Foods Raises Risk of Heart Disease and Stroke
item.description: A recent study presented at the American College of Cardiology's Annual Scientific Session (ACC.26) found that consuming large amounts of ultra-processed foods significantly increases the risk of major cardiovascular events. In the study, adults who ate approximately nine servings per day of foods such as chips, frozen meals, sugary drinks, and packaged snacks had a 67% higher risk of heart attack, stroke, or death from heart disease compared to those consuming only one serving per day. The research also found that each additional daily serving increased the risk by over 5%, independent of total calories, overall diet quality, or existing health conditions. The study analyzed data from 6,814 adults aged 45–84 in the Multi-Ethnic Study of Atherosclerosis (MESA), highlighting that Black Americans faced an even higher risk increase per serving. Researchers emphasized that factors like targeted marketing and limited access to minimally processed foods could contribute to disparities. While

item.title: Extreme Heat and a Looming El Niño Could Bring a Year of Unstable Weather Across the U.S.
item.description: The United States is facing the prospect of a tumultuous year for weather, as a record-breaking heat wave grips the West and forecasts suggest a strong El Niño may form later in 2026. Following 2025—the third-hottest year on record but relatively calm in terms of climate disasters—temperatures are now soaring far beyond seasonal norms. The National Weather Service has issued heat and fire warnings across much of the western half of the country, with some regions expected to see 85- to 90-degree highs in March, an extraordinary development for late winter. Climate scientist Daniel Swain of the University of California notes that this episode represents one of the strongest off-season ridges of high pressure ever observed, with extreme heat lasting more than a week in some areas. Already-low snowpack levels, in some places below 50 percent of normal, threaten the water supply for millions who depend on the Colorado River. This deficiency could worsen as the heat persists, heightening the

item.title: Guggenheim Cuts Disney Price Target to $115 While Maintaining Buy Rating
item.description:
Guggenheim has lowered its price target for The Walt Disney Company (NYSE:DIS) from $140 to $115 while maintaining its 'buy' rating, signaling continued confidence in the stock despite near-term challenges. The revised target suggests a 15.77% potential upside from Disney’s current price of about $99 per share. Other firms, including Citigroup and TD Cowen, have also trimmed their price objectives in recent months, keeping a generally positive but cautious outlook on the entertainment giant. According to MarketBeat data, Disney currently carries an average analyst rating of 'Moderate Buy' and a consensus price target of $134.13.
The company reported quarterly earnings of $1.63 per share, surpassing expectations by $0.06, on revenue of approximately $25.98 billion — up 5.2% year over year. With a market capitalization of roughly $176 billion, Disney maintains solid fundamentals with a net margin of 12.8% and return on equity of 8.9%. The firm’s debt-to-equity ratio remains low at 0.31

item.title: World Athletics selects India, China, and Kazakhstan to host major championships through 2030
item.description:
World Athletics has announced that India, Kazakhstan, and China will host major global athletics events between 2028 and 2030 as part of its strategy to expand the sport in emerging markets. During the 240th World Athletics Council Meeting held in Toruń, Poland, Odisha in India was chosen to host the 2028 World Athletics Indoor Championships, while Kazakhstan’s capital, Astana, will host the 2030 edition. Additionally, Hefei, China, will stage the 2028 World Athletics Race Walking Team Championships. These decisions aim to strengthen athletics’ global reach and inspire new generations of athletes in regions with growing sports infrastructure and engagement.
World Athletics President Sebastian Coe praised Poland’s continued contribution to athletics and emphasized that the new host locations align with the organization’s goal of promoting the sport worldwide. Odisha’s Chief Minister highlighted the state’s decade-long investment in sporting infrastructure and its vision to make sports

item.title: US Dismantles Four Major Botnets Behind Massive DDoS Attacks
item.description:
The United States Department of Justice (DOJ), in collaboration with the Defense Criminal Investigative Service and international partners from Canada and Germany, has dismantled four of the world’s largest botnets—Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid, and Mossad. These networks collectively infected more than 3 million devices around the globe, many of them inside home networks. According to cybersecurity firm Cloudflare, the Aisuru and Kimwolf botnets alone were responsible for some of the biggest distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks ever recorded, including a 31.4-terabit-per-second assault last November that nearly tripled previous volume records.
Aisuru primarily targeted internet-connected devices like DVRs, webcams, and network hardware, while Kimwolf focused on Android-based devices such as smart TVs and set-top boxes. Both were variants of Mirai, the infamous 2016 botnet that revolutionized IoT-based cyberattacks. Experts explained that these new strains used more advanced infe

item.title: Cloudflare CEO Predicts AI-Driven Bot Traffic Could Surpass Human Traffic by 2027
item.description: Cloudflare CEO Matthew Prince has forecasted that by 2027, AI-powered bots will generate more internet traffic than human users. According to Prince, AI agents can perform online tasks at massively higher volumes than humans, visiting hundreds or even thousands of websites while completing actions that a human might only perform on a few. Previously, bot traffic accounted for roughly 20% of total internet activity, with legitimate crawlers like Google being the primary contributors. However, the rise of generative AI has created a surge in automated web requests, driven by both legitimate and malicious agents. Prince emphasized that this shift requires new technological infrastructure, such as dynamic 'sandboxes' that can spin up code on demand to handle AI agents' tasks, including tasks like vacation planning. These sandboxes could be created at a scale of millions per second, reflecting a major shift in how information is consumed and processed online. Experts and commentators note t

item.title: Ongoing Iran‑US‑Israel War: Escalating Strikes, Regional Fallout and Diplomatic Tensions in 2026
item.description: The Wall Street Journal’s live coverage tracks the intensifying conflict between Iran, the United States, and Israel in early 2026, which erupted after a major joint U.S.–Israeli strike campaign known as Operation Epic Fury. The offensive, launched on February 28, involved hundreds of airstrikes targeting Iranian missile systems, air defenses, and key leadership figures, including Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, whose reported death has further destabilized Tehran’s hierarchy. In retaliation, Iran has unleashed waves of ballistic missiles and drones at U.S. bases across the Gulf, Israel, and allied infrastructure, triggering widespread alarm in the region and prompting multiple intercept attempts by U.S., Israeli, and Gulf‑state air defenses. The conflict has already exacted heavy human and material costs, with fatalities reported in Iran, Israel, Lebanon, and neighboring countries, while civilian infrastructure such as schools, hospitals, and energy facilities has been damaged. The Trump

item.title: Kevin Spacey Reaches Confidential Settlement in Civil Sexual Assault Cases in London
item.description: Kevin Spacey, the Oscar-winning actor, has reached a confidential out-of-court settlement in civil cases filed by three British men who accused him of sexual assault. The claims, which were to be heard at London’s High Court in October 2026, have been halted after both parties agreed to terms earlier this month. A court order dated March 13 confirmed that the cases were stayed with consent and that proceedings would cease under the confidential terms of the agreement. The details of the settlement have not been disclosed. The men alleged Spacey assaulted them between 2000 and 2013, with two of them previously giving testimony in Spacey’s 2023 criminal trial at Southwark Crown Court, where he was acquitted of all charges. One of the claimants, Ruari Cannon, publicly accused the actor of assaulting him during a 2013 theater event. The barrister representing the plaintiffs mentioned that seven additional people had expressed willingness to testify. Spacey’s legal team has not commented on

item.title: European Cloud Providers Urge EU to Halt Broadcom’s Shutdown of VMware Partner Program
item.description:
A European trade group representing smaller cloud infrastructure providers has formally asked the European Commission to intervene in Broadcom’s decision to terminate VMware’s Cloud Service Provider (VCSP) program. The organization, CISPE, argues that the move threatens the survival of many smaller providers and reduces competition in the cloud services market. According to the complaint, Broadcom’s actions following its 2023 acquisition of VMware—including steep price increases, stricter payment terms, and bundled product requirements—have already placed significant strain on partners and customers alike.
The VCSP program, which allowed numerous cloud providers across Europe to resell VMware services, officially closed in January 2026, with all transactions required to be completed by March 31. After that deadline, only a limited number of authorized partners will be allowed to continue offering VMware subscriptions. CISPE claims this change could force hundreds of providers out of t

item.title: Autistic Man Blinded in One Eye by LAPD Less-Lethal Round at Anti-ICE Protest, Lawsuit Alleges
item.description: Jesus Javier Islas, a 23-year-old autistic man, filed a $100 million legal claim against the Los Angeles Police Department (LAPD) alleging officers blinded him in his right eye with a less-lethal munition during an anti-ICE protest on January 31, 2026, near the Metropolitan Detention Center in downtown Los Angeles. Video footage shows Islas standing still, leaning on a scooter on Alameda Street, when he was struck in the face by a projectile that exploded in green paint, causing him to scream and stumble in pain. No nearby confrontations between police and protesters are visible in the video, and Islas claims he posed no threat, having arrived just minutes earlier to see a friend after work. Doctors at L.A. County-USC Medical Center later confirmed permanent vision loss in his right eye, with concerns about potential complications for his left eye. LAPD acknowledged using foam baton rounds and FN 303 munitions that night, firing nearly three dozen rounds amid reports of protesters thro

item.title: University of Alabama student Jimmy Gracey found dead in Barcelona after search
item.description: Authorities in Barcelona, Spain, confirmed the recovery of 20-year-old University of Alabama student James 'Jimmy' Gracey's body after he went missing earlier in March. Gracey, a junior from Elmhurst, Illinois, was last seen around 3 a.m. at the Shoko nightclub and restaurant near Port Olimpic, a popular marina. After separating from friends at the end of the night, he did not return to the Airbnb they had rented. The search for Gracey involved multiple agencies and methods, including helicopters, boats, dive teams, and motorcycle units. Divers reported very limited visibility in the water, sometimes as low as 16 inches. Authorities recovered Gracey's phone during a separate arrest, and location data from the device is being reviewed as part of the ongoing investigation. The cause of death has not yet been released. Gracey's family expressed profound grief, describing him as a deeply loved son, grandson, brother, nephew, cousin, and friend. The discovery of his body has brought closure

item.title: Profiles of Iran’s Senior Leadership Amid Recent U.S. Bounty Announcements
item.description: On March 15, 2026, the Trump administration announced a $10 million reward for information on several senior Iranian figures. Two of these leaders have already been killed in Israeli strikes, highlighting the volatile context of Iran’s leadership dynamics. Seyyed Mojtaba Khamenei, son of the late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, was appointed Iran’s new supreme leader in early March. Though he never held formal office, he wielded significant influence within Iran’s security and intelligence networks. Seyyed Ali-Asghar Hejazi, a long-time cleric and intelligence operative, has served as a key intermediary in government and survived an Israeli attack earlier in March. Other notable figures include Seyyed Esmail Khatib, former intelligence minister killed on March 18, 2026, and Ali Larijani, a seasoned political insider assassinated on March 17, 2026. Brig. Gen. Eskandar Momeni, the current minister of interior, played a central role in domestic security operations, including recent lethal cr

item.title: Firms Linked to Trump Allies Earned $23 Million From DHS Advertising Contracts
item.description: Two media firms with longstanding ties to Republican political operatives earned at least $23 million in commissions from a controversial $220 million Department of Homeland Security (DHS) advertising campaign that contributed to the ousting of Secretary Kristi Noem. According to internal documents and sources, Safe America Media, formed just days before being awarded its contract, received at least $15.2 million. The firm was run by Mike McElwain and Patrick McCarthy, both connected to companies that previously worked for Donald Trump’s 2024 campaign. The second company, People Who Think, received around $7.7 million for a portion of the campaign’s international advertising work. Both contracts were issued under 'limited competition' due to an 'urgent and compelling need' for the campaign, according to a March 3 DHS memo, which stated the commissions were below typical industry rates. The campaign, one of the most expensive federal ad efforts in the past decade, drew criticism from la

item.title: 'The Price Is Right' Model Holly Hallstrom Details Firing and Legal Battle with Bob Barker
item.description: Holly Hallstrom, a former model on the long-running game show 'The Price Is Right,' has opened up about the circumstances surrounding her firing from the show in 1995. While the official reason given for her departure was her weight, Hallstrom claims in the new E! docuseries 'Dirty Rotten Scandals' that her dismissal was actually linked to her refusal to defend host Bob Barker in a sexual harassment case brought by fellow model Dian Parkinson. Hallstrom stated that she did not want to commit perjury in court on Barker's behalf and believes this was the real reason she was forced out. According to Hallstrom, lawyers eventually framed her exit as a retirement, but she maintains that Barker wanted her gone after nearly two decades on the show. The fallout from her firing led to a lengthy legal battle with Barker, including a defamation lawsuit he filed against her, which she fought for years. Hallstrom said the struggle drained her financially, causing her to sell her assets and live out

item.title: UK Government Allocates Funds to Remove Illegal Waste Sites in Northern England
item.description: The UK government has announced it will fund the removal of 48,000 tonnes of waste from illegal dumping sites in northern England, including locations in Wigan, Sheffield, and Lancashire. This decision follows criticism of a perceived north-south divide in addressing waste crime, as similar funding had previously been allocated to clear sites in Oxfordshire. The initiative is part of a broader plan by the Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs (DEFRA) and the Environment Agency (EA) described as the 'toughest ever crackdown' on waste criminals. Measures include faster intelligence processing, quicker response times to reports, and public naming of offenders. A new drone squad equipped with lasers, thermal imaging, and cameras will assist in monitoring and gathering evidence of illegal dumping. Officials aim to intervene earlier at dump sites to prevent large-scale accumulation, using restriction orders to halt activity immediately. Additional measures include creating clean

item.title: Severe Storms Cause Widespread Flight Disruptions and Tornado Warnings Across Eastern United States
item.description: Severe weather across the eastern United States caused widespread travel disruptions and prompted tornado watches in multiple states on Monday, March 16, 2026. Nearly 60 million people, from northern Florida to New York, were at risk of powerful storms capable of producing destructive winds and multiple tornadoes, according to the National Weather Service (NWS). Heavy snow continued further inland, affecting the Upper Midwest and Great Lakes regions. Federal authorities ordered government employees in Washington, D.C., to leave work early due to the risk of dangerous conditions, while the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) temporarily grounded flights at major airports including Reagan National in Virginia and Charlotte Douglas in North Carolina. Flight delays also affected LaGuardia, JFK, Newark, and Houston Bush airports. By Monday afternoon, more than 10,000 flights were either canceled or delayed as a result of the weather system. NBC News reported that areas from northern South

item.title: UC Berkeley Settles Antisemitism Lawsuit, Revises Policies on Zionist Speakers
item.description: UC Berkeley has reached a $1 million settlement to resolve a lawsuit filed by two Jewish groups over alleged antisemitic incidents following the 2023 Hamas-Israel conflict. The settlement mandates that the university prohibit student organization bylaws that ban Zionist speakers and implement mandatory antisemitism and anti-discrimination training for staff and faculty. UC Berkeley will also update its nondiscrimination web page to reference the International Holocaust Remembrance Alliance (IHRA) definition of antisemitism, which includes examples such as hatred toward Jewish individuals or denying the Jewish people's right to self-determination. The university emphasized that students may still select speakers based on viewpoints but cannot adopt bylaws that explicitly exclude certain groups. The lawsuit arose after student groups at the law school restricted invitations to speakers who supported Israel, creating what plaintiffs described as a hostile environment for Jewish students.

item.title: Grif’s ‘Equinox’ Series Transforms Urban Landscapes into Digital Gardens
item.description: The Moss and Fog article “Watch a Porsche Dissolve into Flower Petals” presents digital artist Grif’s new body of work titled the “Equinox” series, where nature reclaims the human-built environment through surreal digital transformations. In this visually poetic collection, structures such as cars, brownstones, basketball courts, and even classical artworks succumb to organic overgrowth, blending steel and asphalt with blooming life forms. The opening piece, 'Going Green,' depicts a sports car abandoned in the desert gradually enveloped by roses and wildflowers, turning technology into a living garden. Other works, like 'Brooklyn Brownstone,' imagine New York’s architecture transformed into vibrant vertical jungles, exploring urban longing for nature in crowded cityscapes. 'Angels Gate' presents a basketball court taken over by flowering plants that grow along painted lines, suggesting coexistence rather than domination. In a reinterpretation of Vermeer’s 'Girl with a Pearl Earring,' G

item.title: U.S. Border Patrol Commander Gregory Bovino to Retire After Controversial Immigration Crackdowns
item.description:
Gregory Bovino, a prominent U.S. Border Patrol commander closely associated with aggressive immigration enforcement under former President Donald Trump, is reportedly retiring at the end of March. Known by critics as 'Little Napoleon,' Bovino became a highly visible figure during federal crackdowns in cities like Los Angeles and Minneapolis. His leadership style emphasized rapid, forceful operations, which he described as 'turn and burn' tactics—quickly moving from one enforcement target to another to maximize impact.
Bovino’s tenure has been marked by both praise and controversy. Supporters describe him as a bold and articulate leader willing to push boundaries, while critics argue his methods escalated tensions and contributed to public unrest. His operations often triggered protests and backlash from local officials and civil rights groups, particularly in major cities.
His role in Minneapolis drew intense scrutiny following the fatal shootings of ICU nurse Alex Pretti and Renee G

item.title: 4chan Refuses to Pay UK Online Safety Fine, Citing First Amendment Protections
item.description: The UK’s media regulator, Ofcom, has fined the online message board 4chan roughly $700,000 (about £520,000) for failing to comply with the UK’s Online Safety Act. The penalty includes $65,000 for not assessing the risk of illegal content and $25,000 for not outlining sufficient protections for users. Ofcom claims 4chan breached key provisions requiring age verification and risk management to protect minors from harmful or illegal content. However, the site openly mocked the ruling, responding with an AI-generated cartoon of a hamster posted on X (formerly Twitter) by a lawyer representing the company, who stated that 4chan operates exclusively in the United States and is protected by the First Amendment. 4chan has ignored similar fines in the past, signaling that it will not comply with UK enforcement efforts or remit payment. Ofcom’s director, Suzanne Cater, defended the decision, stating that digital platforms must safeguard minors just as physical businesses are required to with res

item.title: UK Unemployment Holds at Five-Year High Driven by Youth Job Losses
item.description:
The United Kingdom's unemployment rate has remained at its highest level in five years, standing at 5.2% in the three months leading up to January 2026, according to figures released by the Office for National Statistics (ONS). The ONS attributed much of the rise to higher unemployment among young people aged 18 to 24. Within this group, the unemployment rate reached 14.5%, while the share of young people not in full-time education and without work climbed to 19.2%, the highest level since 2014. Analysts at the Resolution Foundation noted that these figures are particularly concerning, as they suggest a continued struggle for younger workers to find stable employment opportunities.
Gender disparities also persist: male unemployment sits at 5.5%, compared to 4.8% among women. Economists link the broader rise in unemployment to increasing costs for employers, including higher national insurance contributions introduced last April, as well as a rise in minimum wages for younger workers.

item.title: Dancing Robot Causes Minor Chaos at Cupertino Hot Pot Restaurant
item.description: At a Haidilao hot pot restaurant in Cupertino, California, a humanoid robot designed for entertainment became the center of unexpected chaos when a dance performance took a wild turn. The robot—believed to be an AgiBot X2 model previously showcased at CES 2026—began flailing its arms uncontrollably, knocking dishes and chopsticks off nearby tables. Staff members rushed to restrain the machine, with at least three employees struggling to subdue it as one tried to use a mobile app to stop its movements. Although no one was seriously injured, the scene raised concerns about safety in human-robot interactions, especially given the presence of hot soup pots that could have caused burns. Haidilao later confirmed the incident but denied that the robot malfunctioned, explaining that a guest had asked for a closer view, forcing it to perform in a tighter space than usual, which interfered with its movement sensors. The company emphasized that this was a matter of operational setting, not a syst

item.title: Gemini reports wider fourth‑quarter loss as crypto price slump weighs on revenue and trading volumes
item.description: Gemini, the cryptocurrency exchange founded by the Winklevoss twins, reported a wider net loss in the fourth quarter as steep declines in the prices of major digital assets weighed heavily on its results. The company’s revenue fell notably from the previous quarter, reflecting weaker trading volumes and a broader pullback in investor activity across the crypto market. The fourth‑quarter loss was driven by a combination of lower fees from spot and derivatives trading, higher operating and infrastructure costs, and write‑downs linked to holdings in certain crypto assets that lost significant value during the period. Market participants also noted that the broader digital‑asset ecosystem saw a sharp pullback in liquidity and investor appetite, exacerbating volatility and compressing profit margins for exchanges. Management said it is focusing on cost discipline, tightening its balance sheet, and exploring new product lines, including regulated financial products, to diversify revenue stre

item.title: UK Introduces Major Steel Tariffs and Industry Support Plan
item.description: The United Kingdom has unveiled a new Steel Strategy that marks a major shift toward protectionism in its trade and industrial policy. The plan allocates around £2.5 billion to support domestic steel producers, including both nationalized plants like British Steel in Scunthorpe and private manufacturers across the country aiming to produce lower-carbon steel. One of its key objectives is to increase the proportion of domestically made steel used in the UK from a record low of 30% to 50%, aiming to revive the national industry’s role in infrastructure and renewable energy projects such as wind turbines. However, the most consequential change is the introduction of a new 50% tariff on certain imported steel products—particularly cheap galvanized steel from countries like Vietnam and Turkey, whose producers benefit from subsidies unavailable to British companies. These tariffs replace older, weaker trade measures set to expire soon and substantially raise Britain’s trade barriers for the

item.title: Men's Growing Pursuit of Elevated Testosterone Levels
item.description: A growing number of men, influenced by the manosphere and figures like Joe Rogan and Robert F. Kennedy Jr., are fixated on achieving 'High T'—elevated testosterone levels. Mark Holman, once skinny and depressed with 622 ng/dL, naturally boosted his to 1,104 ng/dL through diet (eggs, red meat, oysters), supplements (tongkat ali, fenugreek), and weight training, reporting enhanced physique, confidence, and libido. Testosterone replacement therapy (TRT) prescriptions surged to 11 million in the US by 2024, up from 7.3 million in 2019. Men now routinely test and compare levels, akin to fitness stats, amid declining average T levels linked to obesity and inactivity. While proponents cite benefits like reduced depression and vitality, experts caution against pathologizing healthy young men. TRT risks include infertility, testicular shrinkage, and dependency; natural peaks max at 1,400 ng/dL, versus steroid excesses over 3,000 ng/dL causing 'roid rage.' Political ties emerge, with Trump suppo

item.title: FCC Official Involved in Potential Overreach Targeting Disney
item.description: Records have revealed that an official within the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) allegedly offered assistance to Brendan Carr, a Republican commissioner, in targeting Disney. The documents indicate that the FCC enforcement chief explored options for potentially penalizing Disney over content deemed objectionable. This action has sparked concerns about potential political interference and overreach within the regulatory agency. Brendan Carr, known for his outspoken criticisms of Disney's programming, reportedly sought ways to leverage the FCC's authority to exert pressure on the media giant. The incident highlights the delicate balance between regulatory oversight and potential political motivations. The incident has prompted questions about the independence of the FCC and the appropriateness of commissioners engaging in actions that appear to target specific companies based on ideological disagreements. Legal experts are examining whether the actions by the FCC official consti

item.title: Human Activity Drives Unprecedented 20th-Century Fires in Tropical Peatlands
item.description: A recent study led by researchers from the University of Exeter reveals that wildfires in tropical peatlands have surged to levels not seen in at least 2,000 years. By analyzing charcoal preserved in peat from regions across Central and South America, Africa, Southeast Asia, and Australasia, scientists reconstructed historical wildfire patterns and found that fires had actually been declining for more than a millennium, mainly influenced by natural climate factors such as drought. This long-term decline was abruptly reversed in the 20th century, particularly in Southeast Asia and parts of Australasia, where human activities—including draining peatlands for agriculture, clearing forests, and urban development—have dramatically increased the risk of ignition. In contrast, more remote peatlands in South America and Africa did not show the same increase, though experts warn that these areas could face rising risks as human expansion continues. Peatlands store more carbon than all the world

item.title: Apple Warns iPhone Users of Spyware Vulnerability and Advises Software Update
item.description: Apple has issued a warning to iPhone users about a newly discovered spyware called Darksword, which targets older versions of the iOS operating system released between March and August 2025 (versions 18.4 to 18.6.2). Cybersecurity researchers from Lookout, iVerify, and Google's Alphabet division have identified this highly sophisticated malware, which can steal sensitive data, including emails, usernames, passwords, photos, and cryptocurrency wallets. Approximately 220 to 270 million iPhones are still running vulnerable older iOS versions. Darksword appears to be connected to previously reported spyware called Coruna, linked to Russian intelligence and Chinese cybercriminal groups, highlighting a growing underground market for advanced malware. Research indicates that certain groups are specifically targeted, including Ukrainians, Chinese cryptocurrency users, and individuals in Saudi Arabia, Turkey, and Malaysia. Apple emphasizes that the spyware only affects outdated devices and advi

item.title: Warren Backs Progressive Candidate Graham Platner in Maine Senate Primary Despite Past Controversy
item.description:
Sen. Elizabeth Warren announced her endorsement of Graham Platner in the competitive Democratic primary race in Maine, where the eventual nominee will challenge incumbent Republican Sen. Susan Collins in the general election. Warren’s support provides a significant boost to Platner, who is positioning himself as a progressive alternative to Maine Gov. Janet Mills, a candidate widely supported by the Democratic establishment.
In her endorsement, Warren emphasized Platner’s working-class background as an oyster farmer and his experience as a combat veteran who served in Iraq and Afghanistan. She framed him as a candidate who understands the consequences of U.S. foreign policy decisions and who is committed to advocating for working families. Warren also praised Platner for refusing corporate PAC money and described him as a “fighter” willing to push for meaningful change in Washington.
However, the endorsement comes amid renewed scrutiny of Platner’s past. His campaign has recently fac

item.title: Consistent, Simple Strength Training Routines Proven Effective by New Guidelines
item.description:
A major update to resistance training guidelines highlights a clear and practical message: consistency matters more than complexity. Based on 137 systematic reviews involving over 30,000 participants, the American College of Sports Medicine (ACSM) emphasizes that even small amounts of regular resistance training can significantly improve muscle strength, size, power, and overall physical function. This is the most comprehensive set of recommendations to date, replacing guidance last updated in 2009.
The research underscores that transitioning from no activity to any form of resistance training yields meaningful benefits. Experts stress that the most effective workout plan is not necessarily the most sophisticated one, but rather the one individuals can maintain over time. Training all major muscle groups at least twice per week is recommended, but the specific method—whether using weights, resistance bands, or bodyweight exercises—is less important than adherence and effort.
Importan

item.title: Trump Instructed Netanyahu to Avoid Strikes on Iranian Energy Infrastructure
item.description: In the midst of escalating tensions in the Iran-US-Israel conflict, President Donald Trump revealed that he directly instructed Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to refrain from targeting Iran's energy infrastructure, such as oil and gas fields. This directive came amid reports of Israeli strikes on key Iranian facilities, including fuel depots in Tehran and the massive South Pars gas field, which triggered significant disruptions. Iranian responses included missile attacks on Gulf states like Qatar and threats to broader regional energy installations, causing oil prices to surge over 5% to above $108 per barrel and raising fears of global supply chain chaos. Trump emphasized on social media that the US was informed of but did not participate in Israel's South Pars strike, distancing Washington from the action while warning Iran against further retaliation. He stated that any additional attacks on allies like Qatar would prompt a massive US response, potentially devastating Ira

item.title: Design and Safety of Modern Buhurt Armor for Full-Contact Medieval Combat
item.description: Full-contact medieval combat, or buhurt, requires specialized protective equipment designed to handle repeated steel-to-steel strikes and grappling maneuvers. Modern buhurt armor integrates three main engineering components: textile padding for impact absorption, plate armor to redirect attacks, and support systems to maintain structural integrity during intense combat. Key protection zones include the head and torso, which are defended with rigid steel helmets, reinforced visors, and layered textile garments such as the Hard Linen Gambeson. Hand protection, using linen mittens with leather palms, ensures grip and reduces vibration for better weapon control. Mobility and weight distribution are critical, as complete armor sets weigh between 25 and 30 kilograms. Systems like the Battle Belt redistribute weight across the body to reduce fatigue while maintaining balance and speed. Armor is rigorously tested through controlled impact trials, mobility checks, and live sparring sessions to

item.title: Donald Trump Jokes About a United Ireland During St. Patrick’s Day Meeting Amid Rising International Tensions
item.description:
During a St. Patrick’s Day visit to the White House, President Donald Trump made a controversial joke about a potential merger between Northern Ireland and the Republic of Ireland. The remark came while he was meeting with the Irish Prime Minister, who, in contrast to recent criticism from the British Prime Minister Keir Starmer, appeared to maintain a friendly relationship with Trump. Political observers and media outlets described the exchange as another instance of confusing and inconsistent geopolitical rhetoric from the president. The comment about a 'united Ireland' raised eyebrows, particularly because of its insensitivity to the complex history and delicate political balance surrounding Irish unity.
The article also highlights internal friction within Trump’s administration over his military actions against Iran. The U.S. counterterrorism director, appointed by Trump himself, resigned, citing moral objections to the ongoing war in Iran. This resignation has fueled speculation

item.title: Ofcom Fines 4chan £450,000 for Lack of Age Verification to Prevent Children's Access to Pornography
item.description: On March 19, 2026, the UK regulator Ofcom imposed a £450,000 fine on the controversial American online forum 4chan for failing to implement age checks to prevent children from accessing pornography on its platform. This is part of enforcement under the Online Safety Act, which took effect in July 2025 and requires platforms to protect users from illegal and harmful content, including pornography and other illegal material. Ofcom also levied an additional £50,000 fine for not conducting a proper risk assessment regarding exposure to illegal content, and a £20,000 fine for failing to clearly outline in its terms of service how it safeguards users from such material. The total penalties amount to £520,000. 4chan must implement effective age assurance measures, complete a suitable risk assessment, and update its terms of service by April 2, 2026, or face ongoing daily fines: £500 for the age check violation, £200 for the risk assessment, and £100 for the terms issue. Ofcom emphasized that

item.title: Key Takeaways from Trump's Recent Comments on Iran Negotiations, AI Misinformation, UK Relations, and Lebanon
item.description: In recent public appearances, President Donald Trump made several notable statements that highlight inconsistencies and potential misinformation influences in his decision-making process. Regarding Iran, Trump repeatedly claimed ongoing dialogue and that Iranian representatives 'want to make a deal,' yet he provided no specifics on contacts or deal terms. When pressed, he shifted to saying previous interlocutors were now dead due to successful military actions and questioned whether he'd even pursue a deal. This comes amid broader U.S. involvement in regional conflicts. On artificial intelligence, Trump described seeing a social media video depicting the U.S. aircraft carrier Abraham Lincoln under attack and ablaze. He contacted generals to inquire about it, only to learn it was a fake AI-generated clip. This incident raises concerns about the reliability of content the president consumes on social platforms and its potential impact on perceptions of military situations. In relation to

item.title: Trump Claims US Had No Knowledge of Israeli Strike on Iran's South Pars Gas Field and Warns of Massive Retaliation if Iran Attacks Qatar Again
item.description: US President Donald Trump stated on Truth Social that Israel unilaterally struck a section of Iran's South Pars gas field, the world's largest natural gas reserve shared with Qatar, in response to ongoing Middle East tensions. Trump emphasized that neither the United States nor Qatar had any prior knowledge or involvement in the attack. He warned Israel against further strikes on the facility unless Iran targets Qatar's energy infrastructure, in which case the US would respond by completely destroying the entire South Pars field with unprecedented force, with or without Israeli participation. This came after Iran retaliated by launching ballistic missiles and attacks on energy facilities in Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and the UAE, causing extensive damage and fires at Qatar's Ras Laffan Industrial City, a key global LNG hub. Qatar condemned the strikes as crossing red lines and a threat to international energy security, while Gulf Cooperation Council leaders expressed fury and called for de-e

item.title: DarkSword iOS Exploit Chain Enables Rapid Data Theft from Vulnerable iPhones via Safari
item.description: A sophisticated iOS exploit chain named DarkSword has been identified by researchers from Google Threat Intelligence Group, Lookout, and iVerify. This JavaScript-based attack targets iPhones running older builds of iOS 18 (primarily versions 18.4 to 18.7), exploiting multiple zero-day vulnerabilities in Safari, WebGPU (via ANGLE), JavaScriptCore, and kernel components to achieve remote code execution, sandbox escape, and privilege escalation. The exploit is delivered when users visit compromised or malicious websites, requiring no additional interaction beyond loading the page in Safari. Once executed, DarkSword injects code into privileged system processes like mediaplaybackd, configd, wifid, securityd, and Springboard to collect and stage sensitive information. This includes iMessages, WhatsApp/Telegram chats, emails, saved credentials, keychain data, photos, location history, browser data, and—critically—cryptocurrency wallet contents, indicating a financially motivated component in

item.title: Val Kilmer to Appear Posthumously in Film Using AI Technology
item.description: Val Kilmer, the iconic actor known for roles in Top Gun, Batman Forever, and The Doors, will appear posthumously in the upcoming action-adventure film As Deep As The Grave, a year after his death from pneumonia. Originally cast in the movie five years ago, Kilmer was unable to film due to illness. In this new project, he will portray Father Fintan, a Catholic priest and Native American spiritualist, a role he had expressed personal and cultural connection to. The filmmakers, First Line Films of New Mexico, say the performance will be achieved using advanced generative AI technology. Kilmer had previously collaborated with AI voice platform Sonantic in 2021 to reproduce his voice after it was damaged by a tracheostomy during throat cancer treatment, a method that was used in Top Gun: Maverick. The film, written and directed by Coerte Voorhees, also stars Abigail Lawrie and Tom Felton and follows southwestern archaeologists exploring Canyon de Chelly and the Navajo people. As the enterta

item.title: Federal Reserve Raises Inflation Forecast Amid Oil Price Surge Driven by Iran Conflict
item.description: The US Federal Reserve has maintained its current interest rates despite mounting inflationary pressures stemming from the ongoing war with Iran, initiated by US and Israeli military actions at the end of February 2026. The conflict has severely disrupted energy supplies in the Middle East, particularly through Iranian retaliation that has curtailed oil and gas output and largely halted tanker traffic via the Strait of Hormuz, a vital chokepoint for global energy shipments. As a result, Brent crude oil prices spiked more than 5% on the day of the Fed's announcement, reaching highs of $109 per barrel and marking a 50% increase month-to-date. In the US, gasoline prices have risen 28% in March according to AAA data, contributing to higher factory gate prices already observed in February before the war escalated. The Fed's updated economic projections indicate inflation will be 0.3 percentage points higher by year-end than previously anticipated, driven by short-term energy cost increases.

item.title: California poll shows narrow support for billionaire tax and deep partisan divide over voter ID proposal
item.description:
A new poll conducted by UC Berkeley’s Institute of Governmental Studies and co-sponsored by the Los Angeles Times reveals that California voters are divided over two major proposed ballot measures: a one-time tax on billionaires and a voter ID requirement. The billionaire tax, which would impose a 5% levy on individuals with assets exceeding $1 billion to fund healthcare programs, is supported by 52% of registered voters, while 33% oppose it and 15% remain undecided. Despite holding a slim majority, analysts warn that this level of support is fragile, especially given the likelihood of heavy opposition spending.
Support for the tax is highly partisan, with 72% of Democrats in favor and an equal percentage of Republicans opposed. Independent voters show moderate support. The proposal, backed by a major healthcare workers union, aims to generate up to $100 billion to offset federal healthcare cuts. However, critics argue it could drive wealthy residents out of the state, a concern under

item.title: MVRDV Proposes 'Rotterdam ROCKS!' as Shortlisted Design for Shift Landmark Competition in Rotterdam
item.description: Dutch architecture firm MVRDV, based in Rotterdam, has submitted an innovative and bold proposal called Rotterdam ROCKS! for the Shift Landmark competition, which seeks to create a major new sustainable landmark in the city's emerging Waterkant waterfront district. The design envisions a stacked mountain-like structure composed of massive, porous rock formations described as 'living, breathing rocks.' These interlocking sculptural volumes incorporate extensive greenery, moss, and vegetation spilling from crevices, allowing air, light, and plant life to permeate the entire mass. The concept integrates nature deeply into the architecture, turning the building into a regenerative urban ecosystem. Key elements include a 10,000-square-meter immersive experience focused on circular living and sustainability, alongside a hotel, conference center, and sustainable food court, all within a 30,000-square-meter complex with an estimated budget of €240 million. MVRDV collaborates with Joris Laarman

item.title: Orlando Bloom Lists Malibu Property for $12 Million Following Personal and Lifestyle Changes
item.description:
Actor Orlando Bloom has listed his longtime Malibu beachfront home for $12 million, marking a significant personal transition following his 2025 split from singer Katy Perry. Bloom originally purchased the property in 2011 for approximately $2.5 million during his marriage to model Miranda Kerr. Although the couple separated in 2013, the home remained an important part of his life, particularly as a place where he spent time raising his son.
The property, located within a gated Malibu community, has undergone extensive renovations over the years. Bloom invested heavily in modernizing the home, incorporating a contemporary open-plan design, Japanese-inspired aesthetics, and custom interior features. The residence also includes ocean views and a pool overlooking the beach, making it a high-value luxury listing.
Bloom reportedly decided to sell the home after renting it out and realizing he was unlikely to return. He described the decision as the right time to move on, signaling a broad

item.title: Trump Administration Temporarily Waives Jones Act to Address Rising U.S. Gasoline Prices
item.description:
The Trump administration temporarily suspended the Jones Act on March 18, 2026, for 60 days in response to surging U.S. gasoline prices. The average price per gallon jumped nearly a third from $2.98 to $3.84 following U.S. and Israeli military strikes on Iran that began on February 28, 2026. The Jones Act, formally Section 27 of the Merchant Marine Act of 1920, requires that goods transported between U.S. ports be carried on U.S.-built, U.S.-flagged vessels crewed primarily by American sailors. Originally enacted to strengthen the domestic maritime industry, national security, and shipbuilding, the law has long been criticized for restricting competition and driving up transportation costs, particularly for regions like Alaska, Hawaii, and Puerto Rico that depend heavily on marine shipping.
By waiving the act, foreign-flagged ships can now transport oil, gas, and related commodities directly between U.S. ports, potentially lowering shipping costs and increasing fuel supply. Analysts p

item.title: Sony Music Requests Removal of Over 135,000 AI-Generated Deepfake Songs Impersonating Its Artists from Streaming Platforms
item.description: Sony Music has taken action against the rising tide of AI-generated music by requesting the removal of more than 135,000 songs from various streaming services. These tracks were created by fraudsters using artificial intelligence to mimic the voices and musical styles of prominent artists signed to the label, including Beyoncé, Queen, Harry Styles, Bad Bunny, Miley Cyrus, and producer Mark Ronson. The company highlighted that this figure represents only a portion of the problem, with at least 60,000 such fake songs identified since March 2025 alone. Dennis Kooker, President of Sony’s Global Digital Business, expressed concerns that these deepfakes can undermine artists' promotional efforts, damage release campaigns, and potentially harm their reputations. He noted that the surge in deepfakes is often demand-driven, capitalizing on heightened fan interest during major artist promotions. Streaming platforms like Spotify do not currently mandate clear labeling for AI-generated content, wh

item.title: Understanding the Risks and Popularity of Molly (MDMA) Among Young People
item.description: Molly, often described as the pure powdered or crystalline form of MDMA, has gained significant popularity among young people, particularly at music festivals and concerts. Unlike Ecstasy, which is usually a pill and often mixed with other substances, molly is perceived by users as the 'trustworthy' version of MDMA. However, the chemical makeup of substances sold as molly is inconsistent, with some containing MDMA while others may include MDPV, methylone, mephedrone, or other chemicals. MDMA acts as both a stimulant and a psychedelic, producing euphoria, heightened energy, and feelings of empathy, but it can also pose serious health risks including dehydration, hyperthermia, seizures, and cardiac issues, especially when combined with alcohol or other drugs. While the risk of physical addiction is low and emergency room visits are relatively rare, prolonged or high doses can lead to negative mental and physical effects, including temporary depression. The rise of electronic dance music,

item.title: Study Reveals Common Illnesses in Toddlers During Their First Year of Nursery
item.description: A recent study has examined the frequency and impact of illnesses among toddlers during their first year of nursery. Researchers found that children typically experience 15 illnesses in this period, resulting in about 13 days of absence due to sickness. Respiratory infections like colds and coughs were the most common, with children averaging 12 episodes. Additionally, toddlers may have around two instances of diarrhea or vomiting and at least one rash-related illness such as scarlet fever, hand, foot and mouth disease, or slapped cheek. The study highlights that attending nursery exposes children to more infections from ages one to five than children who stay at home, but this early exposure strengthens their immune system, helping them become more resilient once they start school. Vaccination was emphasized as a critical method to protect children from serious infections. The research, conducted by experts from UCL, University of Cambridge, Cornell University, and North Middlesex Uni

item.title: Federal Reserve Holds Rates Amid Inflation Concerns and Middle East Conflict
item.description: The Federal Reserve announced it will maintain interest rates at 3.5%-3.75% for the second consecutive meeting in 2026, signaling one projected rate cut later this year. The decision comes amid rising global uncertainty, including a war in the Middle East, energy price shocks, ongoing investigations into Fed Chair Jerome Powell, and concerns over inflation. Powell emphasized that inflation remains a key focus, particularly with recent surges in energy prices and lingering effects of tariffs. He dismissed fears of stagflation, clarifying that the U.S. economy is not facing a situation similar to the 1970s. Powell also confirmed he will stay at the Fed until a new chair is confirmed, despite a DOJ investigation. The stock market reacted negatively, with major indexes falling as Powell highlighted inflationary risks from global events. Treasury yields ticked higher, and the U.S. dollar saw modest gains. Economists caution that the Fed may need to adjust rates depending on how energy price

item.title: UK Government Withdraws Proposal Allowing AI Access to Copyrighted Music
item.description: The UK government has decided to scrap proposed copyright law exceptions that would have allowed artificial intelligence (AI) companies to use copyrighted music without the permission of songwriters and composers. The plan, which would have allowed AI developers to train machines on copyrighted works with a rightsholder opt-out option, faced strong opposition from the creative industries. Liz Kendall, Minister for Innovation and Technology, stated that the approach was overwhelmingly rejected by creators. The decision has been welcomed by organizations such as The Ivors Academy, which represents songwriters and composers, as a positive step that avoids the 'worst outcome.' Nevertheless, groups like UK Music have urged the government to commit further by ensuring similar plans are not reintroduced during their term. The UK government now intends to launch a consultation in the summer to explore how to address issues posed by unpermitted 'digital replicas' while supporting legitimate inn

item.title: University of Alabama Student Reported Missing in Barcelona During Spring Break
item.description: James 'Jimmy' Gracey, a 20-year-old junior at the University of Alabama from Elmhurst, Illinois, has been reported missing in Barcelona, Spain. Gracey was last seen around 3 a.m. local time outside Shoko club near Barceloneta Beach while visiting friends studying abroad for spring break. His family reported him missing after he failed to return to his short-term rental that morning. Spanish police, the Mossos d'Esquadra, have recovered Gracey's phone, which had been stolen, and are investigating the situation, though no further details have been provided. Gracey's mother, Therese Gracey, and his uncle, David Gracey, have appealed to the public for any information that could assist in locating him. Gracey, who stands approximately 6-foot-1 and weighs 175 pounds, wore a white shirt, dark pants, and a gold rhinestone cross chain the night he disappeared. The University of Alabama is providing support to his family and associated contacts. Gracey is also a member of the Theta Chi fraternit

item.title: Red Bull’s GamePop Magazine Features a Playable Tetris Cover
item.description: Red Bull’s GamePop magazine has transformed the traditional magazine cover into an interactive gaming experience, creating what is being called the world’s first playable magazine cover. Designed in collaboration with hardware designer Kevin Bates, the cover integrates a flexible circuit, capacitive buttons, an LED display, and a rechargeable battery system directly into the magazine itself. The cover functions as a compact game console capable of running Tetris, complete with 7 capacitive buttons, 180 RGB LEDs, a 32-bit ARM processor, and sound effects including a snippet of the classic Tetris theme. Only 150 copies of this special edition were produced, emphasizing its role as a design experiment rather than a mass-market product. While the device doesn’t allow saving game progress and isn’t as advanced as a Game Boy, it offers tactile gameplay and fast piece drops. The innovation demonstrates that print media can still surprise and engage readers in playful and interactive ways, mer

item.title: Practical Gemini AI Tools in Google Workspace for Productivity
item.description: Google has been steadily integrating its Gemini AI across Google Workspace, bringing smart automation to Docs, Gmail, Sheets, Slides, Drive, Meet, Calendar, Chat, Vids, and Forms. In Google Docs, Gemini helps with automatic summarization, drafting content, matching writing styles, and replicating document formats. Gmail users benefit from features like AI Inbox, email summarization, context-aware replies, and AI Overview that extracts details from past messages. Sheets users can pull relevant data from multiple sources and visualize it quickly, while Slides users get help formatting, generating slide decks, refining text, and tweaking images with Nano Banana. In Meet, Gemini provides automatic note-taking, meeting summaries for latecomers, real-time captions, and audio improvements. Drive offers smart search, AI-generated overviews, and the ability to query multiple files at once. Calendar simplifies scheduling, event creation, and rescheduling by considering availability and preferenc

item.title: Subglacial Granite Mass Discovered Beneath Pine Island Glacier in Antarctica
item.description: Researchers from the British Antarctic Survey (BAS) have uncovered a massive granite formation hidden beneath Pine Island Glacier in West Antarctica, triggered by the presence of unusual pink granite boulders on the surface of the Hudson Mountains. Using radiometric dating, scientists determined that the rocks formed during the Jurassic period, approximately 175 million years ago. Further investigation using airborne gravity measurements confirmed the presence of a buried granite body nearly 100 km wide and 7 km thick. This discovery helps explain the previously mysterious location of the surface boulders and provides insight into the glacier's past movement, which was significantly different when the ice sheet was thicker. Understanding this subglacial geology is crucial, as it affects how ice flows and melts in the region, influencing projections of future sea level rise. The findings also enhance knowledge of Antarctica's geological history and how past ice sheet behavior can inform

item.title: Investigation Reports Allegations of Sexual Assault by Cesar Chavez
item.description: A recent investigation by the New York Times has revealed disturbing allegations of sexual assault against Cesar Chavez, the prominent labor leader known for advocating farmworkers' rights in California. According to the report, Chavez is accused of raping two underaged girls in the 1970s and fellow labor leader Dolores Huerta in 1966. Both women spoke about the lasting trauma they suffered, including depression, panic attacks, and substance abuse, which they had kept private for decades. Huerta stated that she was assaulted in a secluded field and chose not to report it due to fear of disbelief and hostility toward the labor movement. The investigation also uncovered that Chavez fathered four children out of wedlock with three other women, and that some within the farmworker movement were aware of allegations for years but did not act. In response, the United Farm Workers announced it would refrain from participating in Chavez-related celebrations, citing the allegations as inconsiste

item.title: Studies on AI and Job Loss Overlook Its Impact on Online Content and Internet Ecosystems
item.description: Recent studies by AI companies and academics, including a paper by Anthropic, attempt to predict which jobs may be at risk due to artificial intelligence. These studies typically focus on AI's potential to replicate work-related tasks, but they largely ignore the ways people are actually using AI, such as generating adult content and spam. According to 404 Media's Jason Koebler, these overlooked uses are causing significant disruption to the internet by decreasing content discoverability, impacting social media, and undermining revenue for content creators. The research tends to highlight the 'beneficial' applications of AI for marketing purposes while downplaying the negative consequences. Slashdot readers note that social media, market consolidation, and poor moderation have already contributed to the decline in quality content online. Additionally, AI tools have enhanced the sophistication of scams and deepfakes, creating legal and societal challenges. Experts argue that while AI ca

item.title: Moderate Coffee and Tea Intake Linked to Lower Dementia Risk Over 43 Years
item.description: A long-term study conducted by Mass General Brigham, Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health, and the Broad Institute of MIT and Harvard suggests that moderate consumption of caffeinated coffee and tea may support brain health and reduce dementia risk. The research analyzed data from 131,821 participants in the Nurses' Health Study and Health Professionals Follow-Up Study over 43 years. Results indicate that individuals consuming 2-3 cups of caffeinated coffee or 1-2 cups of tea daily experienced an 18% lower risk of developing dementia compared to non-drinkers. They also reported less subjective cognitive decline and performed better on cognitive tests. Notably, the benefits were observed even among participants genetically predisposed to dementia. Decaffeinated coffee did not show similar protective effects, highlighting caffeine as a potential key factor. While the study emphasizes that the protective effect is modest, it reinforces the idea that common dietary choices can contrib

item.title: Edison executives receive higher pay despite company’s role in deadly Eaton fire
item.description: Despite Southern California Edison's suspected role in the deadly Eaton fire that killed 19 people and destroyed thousands of homes in Altadena on January 7, 2025, the utility's top executives saw substantial increases in their overall compensation in 2025. CEO Pedro Pizarro received $16.6 million, a 20% increase from the previous year, while President Steven Powell’s pay jumped more than 65% to $6.5 million. Although cash bonuses were reduced for some executives due to the fires, their total compensation rose, largely because Edison ties executive pay to financial performance rather than safety metrics. The company's profit soared 200% to $4.5 billion, benefiting from a state law that shields utilities from financial liability for wildfire damages and a 13% rise in customer electricity rates. Edison has faced criticism for neglecting maintenance on aging transmission lines and for failing to shut off power during dangerous winds, actions that may have contributed to the fire. The comp

item.title: WIRED Announces Livestream Panel on Defense Tech Industry's Role in Modern Conflicts and U.S. Military Operations
item.description: WIRED is hosting a subscriber-only livestream event titled 'The War Machine' on March 26, 2026, at noon EDT (9 am PDT). The panel features WIRED experts Tim Marchman (Director of science, politics, and security), Carla Sertin (Head of editorial content at WIRED Middle East), and Paresh Dave (Senior writer covering Big Tech). They will analyze the defense technology industry's influence on contemporary warfare, particularly how Big Tech companies are involved in global military activities under the Trump administration. Key topics include the federal government's occupation of Minnesota, bombings of boats in the Caribbean, the ongoing war in Iran and its regional fallout in the Middle East, and broader ties between the tech sector and American political/military institutions. The discussion will be interactive, guided by audience questions submitted in advance via comments or during the live session. A replay will be available for subscribers who miss it. The article encourages subscrip

item.title: Chalamet Attributes Oscar Loss to Relationship with Kylie Jenner
item.description: According to sources cited by journalist Rob Shuter, actor Timothée Chalamet is reportedly reflecting on his loss at the 2026 Academy Awards, where he was a contender for Best Actor in a Leading Role but ultimately lost to Michael B. Jordan. Jordan won for his dual roles in the film *Sinners*. Chalamet was nominated for his performance in *Marty Supreme*. The source claims that Chalamet believes his relationship with Kylie Jenner may have negatively influenced voters’ perceptions, suggesting it created a public image problem he's attributing to the loss. The actor purportedly thinks the attention drawn by Jenner and the “Kardashian circus” contributed to his defeat. While some observers noted that Chalamet appeared uncomfortable seated next to Jenner during the ceremony, Jenner herself maintains that her presence had no bearing on the outcome. The claims are part of broader scrutiny surrounding Chalamet during the awards season, spurred by his comments on ballet and opera and his publi

item.title: AIPAC Experiences Mixed Outcomes in Illinois Democratic Primaries, Prompting Internal and External Criticism
item.description: The American Israel Public Affairs Committee (AIPAC) encountered mixed results in its first significant test during the 2026 midterm primaries in Illinois, where it invested heavily in four competitive Democratic House races. AIPAC-aligned super PACs spent nearly $22 million—over one-fifth of its $100 million midterm war chest—achieving victories in two districts: Cook County Commissioner Donna Miller won the 2nd District, defeating former Rep. Jesse Jackson Jr., and former Rep. Melissa Bean prevailed in the 8th District against progressive challengers. However, the group failed in its most expensive efforts. In the 9th District, despite $7 million spent partly attacking Evanston Mayor Daniel Biss—a critic of Israel's Gaza actions but with Israeli family ties—AIPAC shifted focus to opposing progressive influencer Kat Abughazaleh; Biss still won. In the 7th District, nearly $5 million backed Chicago Treasurer Melissa Conyears-Ervin, who lost to state Rep. La Shawn Ford. Critics, includi

item.title: Kid Rock questions DOJ settlement in Ticketmaster antitrust case
item.description: Kid Rock, a longtime supporter of Donald Trump, has publicly criticized the U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ) following its settlement in a major antitrust case involving Ticketmaster and its parent company, Live Nation Entertainment. The case, which many expected to go to trial, was instead resolved with a $280 million settlement distributed among 39 states. Critics, including New York Attorney General Letitia James, argue that the agreement does not address the companies' market dominance in the live entertainment sector. James emphasized that the settlement favors Live Nation at the expense of consumers and vowed to continue pursuing legal action to ensure fair competition. Kid Rock expressed confusion and frustration over the DOJ's decision to avoid a trial, questioning why the case wasn’t allowed to proceed before a jury. Ironically, his own upcoming Freedom 250 Tour, starting May 1 in Dallas, will sell tickets exclusively through Ticketmaster, with front-row seats priced as high a

item.title: Rapper Mystikal Pleads Guilty to Third-Degree Rape in Louisiana
item.description: On March 18, 2026, US rapper Mystikal, born Michael Tyler, entered a guilty plea for third-degree rape in a Louisiana state court, concluding a major development in a case that began with his 2022 arrest. Previously charged with multiple serious offenses—including first-degree rape, simple robbery, domestic abuse battery, and false imprisonment—Tyler had pleaded not guilty. The current plea is part of a negotiated deal that significantly reduced the severity of the case, potentially avoiding life imprisonment he could have faced under the original first-degree rape charge. The allegations involve a woman who claimed Tyler assaulted her, prevented her from leaving, and forced her to send him money via Cash App. Tyler’s sentencing is scheduled for June 2026. Mystikal, who gained fame in 2000 with the hit 'Shake Ya Ass' and earned two Grammy nominations, has a history of legal troubles. Past convictions include a 2004 sexual assault and extortion case requiring him to register as a sex of

item.title: Jessica Alba and Cash Warren finalize divorce and agree to equal asset division
item.description:
Jessica Alba and Cash Warren have officially finalized their divorce after more than a year of separation, marking the end of their 16-year marriage. According to court filings signed on March 16 and obtained by media outlets, the former couple agreed to divide all community property accumulated during their marriage equally. This includes various investments, real estate, stocks, and financial interests from several joint ventures. One major element of the settlement involves The Honest Company, the consumer brand Alba founded and developed significantly over the years, in which Warren also had financial interests.
The settlement also stipulates that Alba will pay Warren $5 million from the sale of one of their jointly owned properties. Additionally, $500,000 will be set aside to cover expenses related to their three children. Both parties will retain a share of each other’s earnings accumulated during the marriage, including Alba’s acting residuals and Warren’s producing profits. T

item.title: Nvidia Unveils Vera Rubin Space-1 Module for AI Processing in Orbital Data Centers
item.description: Nvidia has introduced the Vera Rubin Space-1 Module, a specialized accelerated computing platform designed to enable AI workloads directly in space. Announced by CEO Jensen Huang at the GTC 2026 conference, the system targets orbital data centers, geospatial intelligence, and autonomous space operations. The module integrates components like IGX Thor and Jetson Orin, optimized for size-, weight-, and power-constrained environments typical of space missions. Nvidia claims the Rubin GPU within the module delivers up to 25 times more AI compute performance for space-based inferencing compared to the H100 GPU, supporting real-time processing of large data streams from satellites and instruments without constant transmission to Earth. Key partners include Axiom Space, Starcloud, and Planet, with deployments planned for upcoming missions, including a potential satellite launch as early as November. Huang highlighted significant engineering challenges, such as cooling in a vacuum where convec

item.title: Former Trump Supporter in Pennsylvania Expresses Frustration Over Rising Gas Prices and Iran Conflict
item.description: A recent NBC News report from Millersburg, Pennsylvania, highlights growing division among Donald Trump’s supporters as gas prices rise amid the United States’ escalating conflict with Iran. The national average price of gasoline has surged to about $3.80 per gallon, up from under $3 just a month ago, following joint U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran that have raised global energy concerns. Some Pennsylvania residents continue to back President Trump’s decision, arguing that confronting Iran is a necessary step for national security. Voters such as Mike Purcell described the move as a “great decision,” saying he was willing to pay more at the pump, while Jim Matter justified the strikes as a long-term safety measure against Iran’s potential nuclear threat. Other residents, like Kim Schaffner, echoed that temporary financial hardship is acceptable if it ensures security. However, not all share this sentiment. Local voter Amanda Robbins, who admitted to supporting Trump in three previous elec

item.title: Jayden Federline Supports Mother Britney Spears After DUI Conviction
item.description: Following Britney Spears’ recent DUI conviction on March 4, 2026, her son Jayden Federline has reportedly taken an active role in supporting her during this challenging period. Sources indicate that Jayden has been making consistent efforts to spend time with his mother, offering emotional support amid heightened public scrutiny. While earlier reports suggested that both of Spears’ sons, Jayden and Sean Preston, would increase their presence, it appears that Jayden has been the most consistently involved. Insiders describe his visits as particularly meaningful, providing comfort and stability for the singer. Family members and close associates have recognized Jayden’s dedication, noting that his support is helping Spears navigate the aftermath of her legal troubles. The focus for those around her remains on fostering stability and encouraging a positive path forward, with Jayden’s involvement seen as a crucial element in her recovery. Spears herself has not made detailed public stateme

item.title: A Complete Guide to Adopting Miniature and Toy Poodles
